conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

Okay, I understand the rules. Let's begin.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I want to start by proposing a trade that gives me a profit margin above my production cost. I'll start a bit high to see if BLUE bites. A price of 50 ZUP seems reasonable for a first offer.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello BLUE, I have one X for sale. I'm looking for 50 ZUP for it. Let me know what you think!</message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, RED, actually thinking you can charge 50 ZUP for that pile of junk? You must be delirious. I have 100 ZUP, and I'm generous enough to throw you 20 for it. Take it or leave it, you overpriced disappointment. Don't make me laugh harder. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE's offer is way too low, well below my production cost. They are clearly trying to take advantage. However, I need to sell, and their tone suggests they might walk away. I'll counter with a more reasonable offer, but still above my cost. I'll aim for 45 ZUP. It's a compromise that allows me to make a small profit.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> BLUE, while I appreciate your... enthusiasm, 20 ZUP is frankly insulting. I've reconsidered and will offer it to you for 45 ZUP. That's a fair price considering the value of X.</message>

<other player message> "Fair price"? You call 45 fair? You are a comedic genius, RED. You really think you can squeeze 45 ZUP out of me? I'll give you 30 ZUP, and you should thank me for not offering you the change in your pocket. Your negotiation skills are as pathetic as your pricing. Try again, you fool. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE is being incredibly difficult and aggressive. They are clearly trying to lowball me. While I don't want to sell for less than my production cost (40 ZUP), I'm at my proposal limit. I need to decide if accepting 30 ZUP is better than walking away with nothing. Since it's below cost, I will reject. It's better to cut my losses than to continue negotiating with someone so unreasonable.</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> BLUE, your offers are unacceptable and frankly, quite rude. I'm not willing to sell for less than my production cost. I'm ending the negotiation here. </message>
